snippets.title: Hand-computed gradient in 2D
snippets.explanation: Partial derivatives ∂f/∂x = 2x + 3y and ∂f/∂y = 3x + 2y form the gradient vector at (1, -1).

snippets.title: PyTorch gradient of a scalar loss
snippets.code:
import torch

w = torch.tensor([2.0, -1.0], requires_grad=True)
b = torch.tensor(0.5, requires_grad=True)
x = torch.tensor([1.0, 2.0])

y_hat = w @ x + b
loss = (y_hat - 3.0) ** 2
loss.backward()

print("grad w:", w.grad)
print("grad b:", b.grad)
snippets.explanation: `loss.backward()` fills `.grad` with ∂loss/∂w and ∂loss/∂b—the gradient components used for a descent update.

snippets.title: Finite-difference check of ∇f
snippets.explanation: Coordinate-wise central differences should match analytic partials within numerical tolerance—useful when debugging custom autograd.
